c74object.name: dk.shatter~
c74object.digest: Decompose a signal into components using NMF
c74object.description: The dk.shatter~ object takes audio input and decomposes it uses finely-tuned spectral contours that are optimised through machine learning. You can either seed bases directly in the object or load/read ones previous created with dk.shattercreate~.
c74object.discussion:
If you want to learn more about the underlying algorithms have a look at these articles:
https://learn.flucoma.org/reference/nmffilter
https://learn.flucoma.org/reference/nmfmatch

outlet.description: Activation envelope output as multi-channel signal

objarg.name: default
objarg.digest: JSON file to load by default
objarg.description: JSON file to load by default.

method.name: print
method.digest: Print summary info to Max console
method.description: Prints a summary of the analysis to the Max console.

method.name: read
method.description: Reads a pre-decomposed shatter .json file directly from a relative or absolute path. With no additional argument, a file dialog will appear to choose a .json file.

method.name: seed
method.digest: Enable recording for the internal NMF decomposition buffer~
method.description: Enable recording for the internal NMF decomposition buffer~.

attribute.name: attack
attribute.description: Attack time of activation envelope (in ms).

attribute.name: components
attribute.digest: Amount of components for the internal seeding
attribute.description: Amount of components for the internal seeding (not required when loading an analysis).

attribute.name: decay
attribute.description: Decay time of activation envelope (in ms).

attribute.name: fftsettings
attribute.description:
FFT settings consist of three numbers representing the window size, hop size and FFT size in samples:
The hop size and fft size can both be set to -1 (and are by default), with slightly different meanings:
For the hop size, -1 = windowsize/2
For the FFT size, -1 = windowsize snapped to the nearest equal / greater power of 2 (e.g. windowsize 1024 => fftsize 1024, but windowssize 1000 also => fftsize 1024)
Constraints
FFTSize, if != -1, will snap to the next greatest power of two
if FFT size != -1, then window size is clipped at FFT size

attribute.name: history
attribute.digest: The size of the history window for smoothing the list output
attribute.description: The size of the history window for smoothing the list output.

attribute.name: transpose
attribute.digest: Amount to shift the spectral contours over
attribute.description: Amount to shift the spectral contours over (0-100%).
